article.body_preview:
DHAKA, Jan 12, 2023 (BSS) - Bangladesh is significantly prone to many of the

recessionary risks if appropriate steps are not taken to diversify the export

products and basket and increase remittances through the formal channels

although the country may not go into recession.

The International Chamber of Commerce-Bangladesh (ICCB) in its latest

editorial today also highlighted the importance of taking appropriate

measures to streamline public sector expenditures, rationalise mega

infrastru

llm_connections.evidence_quote: will spur capital outflows from the developing countries forcing them to devalue their currencies
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly states developing countries will be 'forced to devalue their currencies', which describes country-level exchange-rate movements matching the feature's exchange-rate volatility scope.

verification.quote_best_match: will spur capital outflows from the developing countries forcing them to devalue their currencies,
verification.judge_reasoning: The article contains the sentence 'The likely recession in the developed world will spur capital outflows from the developing countries forcing them to devalue their currencies...' which explicitly links a (likely) recession to capital outflows that force country-level currency devaluations, matching the exchange-rate (currency) scope.
